metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
As an Account Development Specialist (End-User), you will be on the front lines of customer engagement — identifying, contacting, and selling to new and existing end-user customers and regionally based Engineering firms who could benefit from our products or services. This role is ideal for someone who thrives on direct outreach, enjoys helping people solve problems, and wants to build foundational sales skills in a fast-paced environment. This role is responsible for driving sales of all relevant end-user-focused products by combining a blend of technical knowledge, strategic thinking, and exceptional communication skills. The Account Development Specialist works closely with our Channel and Field Application Engineering teams to ensure customer satisfaction and achieve sales targets.  This role plays a pivotal part in the development of accounts, with the goal of crossing a threshold to where developed accounts are then assigned to Sales Engineers. This position is an individual contributor role and does not include formal people management responsibilities.  

	COMPETENCY PROFILE: 
•	Global Competencies: Collaborate; Deliver Results; Develop Yourself; Drive Customer Value; Improve and Innovate; Take Ownership

RESPONSIBILITES:
•	Meet or exceed territory sales quotas as established by the RSM and/or Vice President—Field Sales.
•	Be proficient in BASE selling skills and implement to build strong customer relationships.
•	Generate leads through cold calls, emails, and networking events.
•	Support the preparation of proposals, presentations, and sales materials
•	Maintain and update CRM systems with accurate client information. Fully utilize the CRM as a communication, reporting, measurement, and tracking tool to enhance productivity.
•	Collaborate with the entire Phoenix Contact team to align efforts, effectively communicate both verbally and written.
•	Track and report on business development activities and performance metrics.
•	Differentiate Phoenix Contact from other component vendors by building “partner” relationships with customers.
•	Focus on the customer by averaging a minimum of 20 sales calls per week
•	Be proficient in and drive the use of all sales tools provided to support local markets.
•	Work closely with Harrisburg Product teams to aid in the development of field presentations and application notes.
•	Proactively utilize all resources and sales tools to maximize productivity, responsiveness, and customer delight.
•	Continue personal development through an effective skill-building program as determined by the RSM, Director of Sales, and/or the Harrisburg Training Department.
•	Demonstrate technical competence on all products/solutions within the area of focus.
•	Provide forecasts and reports to RSM as required.
•	Independently and/or in conjunction with regional management, develop and implement effective Mutual Action Plans with assigned distributor locations.
•	Deliver presentations and hands-on demonstrations of Phoenix Contact products, applications, and technology for the purpose of product sales.
•	Develop and maintain a working knowledge on all Phoenix Contact products/solutions.
•	Qualify and follow up on all leads within the assigned territory.
•	Follow up on all quotations within the assigned territory for the given account portfolio.
•	Other duties as assigned.

QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Four-year degree preferred.
•	Technical discipline preferred and/or equivalent experience.
•	1 to 3 years minimum sales experience in the electronics or industrial automation & controls marketplace.
•	Experience working with distribution preferred.
•	Comfortable making cold calls and sending outreach emails.
•	Self-motivated with the ability to learn and adapt quickly.
•	Demonstrates a willingness to collaborate with others, build relationships, and involve others in decision making. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to deliver results by solving or supporting others in solving problems, prioritizing tasks, and/or resources, and focusing on outcomes. 
•	Demonstrates self-awareness and the willingness to learn and grow, accept changes, and effectively adapt to changing work demands. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to drive customer value through knowledge of the business, putting effort into identifying customer needs, and delivering work to maximize the value for customers. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to promote the value of improvement and innovation by staying ahead of trends, creating ideas and opportunities, and generating speed. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to take ownership by making decisions, taking responsibility, and driving things forward. 



ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	Must be able to travel via airplane.
•	Must have a valid driver’s license and be able to travel via automobile for up to six hours at a time.
•	Must be able to lift a minimum of 50 lbs.
•	Must be able to travel overnight 35-40% or as dictated by assigned territory.
•	Comply with company dress code guidelines.
•	Punctuality is required.
•	Regular and on-time attendance (Must Be Included as Worded Here by Legal).

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
SYNOPSIS:
The building automation engineer will be a self-motivated individual capable of managing our building automation architecture.  As part of our facility infrastructure team this individual will expand and enhance our system and technology for the US facility operations. This position will be responsible for designing, installing, troubleshooting and repairing devices, fixtures, and panels related to the BAS control equipment.  This individual will collaborate with other facilities employees, engineers, and contractors to provide efficient and cost-effective facilities.


RESPONSIBILITIES:
•	Monitor and maintain building automation systems (BAS) including HVAC, lighting, and access control for optimal performance. 
•	Program and configure control sequences to improve energy efficiency and operational reliability. 
•	Respond to system alarms and troubleshoot issues remotely or on-site to minimize downtime. 
o	Including some off hours
•	Optimize climate control and setback schedules to reduce utility costs and support sustainability goals. 
•	Track and analyze energy consumption and self-generation data to identify trends and opportunities for cost savings. 
•	Prepare and submit accurate energy usage reports to headquarters for compliance and corporate sustainability goals. 
•	Develop and implement energy optimization strategies to reduce utility costs and improve efficiency across facilities. 
•	Monitor performance of energy systems
•	 Manage remote facility control for emergency response and operational continuity. 
•	Document system changes, maintenance activities, and configurations for compliance and reporting. 
•	Coordinate with facilities, operations, and vendors for repairs, upgrades, and integration projects. 
•	Ensure OT cybersecurity measures are in place for BAS and related systems to protect critical infrastructure.
•	Remain in compliance with current National Electric Code & NFPA 70E



QUALIFICATIONS:
•	B.S Degree in electrical engineering, computer science or comparable
•	3-5 years minimum work experience w/ Building Automation Systems
•	2-4 years working in Facilities Management environment
•	Experience w/ Siemen’s Desigo and/or Niagara AX systems preferred
•	Strong knowledge of thermodynamics, heat transfer, electrical and electronic engineering as it applies to HVAC controls & Building Automation Systems
•	Strong knowledge of network types and protocols
•	Ability to perform basic BAS programming: function libraries, Java, and IEC61131 preferred
•	Demonstrates extensive knowledge with HVAC and electrical systems
•	Ability to read electrical one line drawings / schematics and mechanical drawings/specs
•	Good decision making and project management skills.
•	Proficiency of Microsoft Outlook, Word and Excel 
•	Able to work under pressure and prioritize multiple tasks
•	Develops team members through mentoring and coaching in complex subjects.
•	Strong communication skills 
•	Strong self-motivator   
•	Displays proficiency in the following global competencies:
o	Initiative – the understanding of what action needs to be taken and completing these tasks while seeking the perspective of others.
o	Comprehensive Thinking (Personal Credibility) – taking personal responsibility for mistakes; does what he/she commits to and is honest and forthright.
o	Collaboration – works as a team player to make a difference within the business and expresses gratitude and appreciation to others who have provided information, assistance and support
o	Willingness to Learn – handles change through taking advantage of lifelong learning opportunities and is open minded and curious to face new challenges.


ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:

•	Domestic travel required 
•	Must be able to stand for extended periods of time
•	Must be able to lift 60 pound
•	Must be able to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends and overtime when necessary.
•	Must be able to handle extreme temperatures.
•	Must be able to speak and understand English to communicate verbally and in writing
•	Must have above average math and statistical analytical skills

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
Internship Summary
Are you looking for an internship that provides real life experience? 
Phoenix Contact’s IMA AI Corporate Management Team is looking for a Business/Data Analyst to assist with data analysis in cross-functional teams.
 Our Spring interns work part time. The intern has the possibility to continue the internship into Summer and Fall 2026.

What does a typical day look like?
On-site day: Meet with Product Managers or AEP team to review data asks for the week. Pull Salesforce reports, clean datasets, prepare Excel or Power BI dashboards. Join a stand-up meeting with cross-functional teams (Product, Sales, Marketing). Work on pricing or customer segmentation projects using ZoomInfo & internal data. Build presentation-ready visuals for leadership. 

Remote day: Deep work: data mining, dashboard building, AI-assisted analysis. Weekly sync via Teams for progress check-ins. Prepare slides summarizing trends and insights. Some details of projects: Salesforce Reporting & Analytics Opportunities Industries / Markets PDNs/PCNs linked to customer trends Manual lead nurturing analysis Data Cleansing / Reporting Streamlining. Pricing Analysis (ground‑up if needed) ZoomInfo data integration for customer acquisition insights Cross Master data cleanup Website Master Data cleanup (Esprit data gaps analysis). Support PM dashboard analysis (margin, PdFI, etc.)
What makes this internship unique?
Phoenix Contact is a private, global leader with a family-like culture. Interns get exposure to real enterprise data, not “sandbox projects”. You’ll work cross-functionally with Product, Sales, and AEP, building a real portfolio. Strong interns are often considered for full-time data analysts or product analyst roles. You’ll learn industry-leading reporting and AI-driven analysis practices

Must-Have Qualifications
·	IT, Marketing, AI Major
·	Proficiency in Excel, Power BI, or Tableau 
·	Strong knowledge of SQL and database fundamentals 
·	Experience using AI productivity tools (Copilot, ChatGPT, etc.) 
·	Coursework in: Data Visualization, Statistical Modeling, Predictive Analytics, Information Systems, 
·	Python/R 
 
Must Be Able To
·	Look at a computer screen for extended periods of time (up to 4 hours)
·	Sit and/or stand for extended periods of time (2-4 hours at a time)
·	Commute to Harrisburg, PA.

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
SYNOPSIS:

This position will lead the administration of specific aspects of order processing and maintenance for new direct customers as well as specific customer accounts.  This position will act as a liaison among external customers and various Phoenix Contact teams in order to successfully fulfill customer expectations and achieve customer satisfaction.

RESPONSIBILITIES:

•	Provide answers to questions through inbound calls, e-mails, and chats from customers and sales team
•	Document customer inquiries in CRM system to provide customer and sales team detailed summary of required actions needed to meet customer needs 
•	Collaborate and communicate with other Phoenix Contact stakeholders to provide unified service experience 
•	Meet job specific service and training goals, while following work processes as defined by management 
•	Make time for and utilize training resources to continually improve job skills and knowledge. 
•	Continue to build a working knowledge of any customer demographics and industry standards and how they apply to specific inquiries
•	Understand why the customer is asking their questions and learn how to proactively address their next set of needs before they ask 
•	Build a working knowledge of applicable policies and procedures, and their intent 
•	Learn key stakeholders in other departments  
•	Participate in Onboarding plan with key stakeholders
•	Facilitate Onboarding discussions and meetings with customers
•	Effectively communicate with customers to understand their needs and provide appropriate solutions 
•	Demonstrate caring, individualized attention to Customers and their needs
•	Perform services both dependably and accurately
•	Demonstrate a willingness to help Customers and provide prompt service
•	Ensure special Customer-specific requirements for order processing, maintenance, and shipping are coordinated and performed
•	Complete Customer-initiated reporting requests
•	Process Customer purchase orders using both EDI, Fax2EDI and manual entry 
•	Analyze data to perform advanced order maintenance and notify Customers of order changes
•	Initiate and maintain stocking agreements
•	Process requests for schedule changes, order changes, and cancellations
•	Process pricing and order expedite requests
•	Process requests for order and inventory status 
•	Maintain Customer item cross-reference tables in the ERP system
•	Participate in business reviews and other Customer-initiated meetings when required
•	Escalates issues when necessary
•	Special projects as assigned by the Extended Services Supervisor

QUALIFICATIONS:

•	Associates degree in Business, logistics, or related field and two years of experience in a customer facing role.  5 years of experience may be considered in lieu of degree
•	Ability to analyze and anticipate situations, define problems and objectives, recognize alternatives, formulate solutions and implementation strategies
•	Ability to exercise flexibility, initiative, sound judgment and discretion.
•	Ability to meet deadlines and adjust to changing priorities to meet business goals
•	Proficient computer skills (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
•	Effective interpersonal skills with comfort in delivering group presentations
•	Preferred 2 years of experience in a business to business sales environment
•	Willingness and ability to adapt to changing departmental needs including but not limited to, schedule changes 
•	Excellent organizational, time management, customer service and problem-solving skills while working in an environment with frequent interruptions 
•	Excellent oral/written English communication skills 
•	Must be able to work with others, build relationships and involve others in decision making.   
•	Motivated self-starter with the ability to work autonomously and as part of a team 
•	Exhibits a working knowledge of policies and procedures
•	Demonstrated ability to accept feedback and make appropriate changes
•	Demonstrated a basic knowledge of customer demographics and industry standards
•	Displays proficiency in the following global competencies:
o	Initiative – the understanding of what action needs to be taken and completing these tasks while seeking the perspective of others.
o	Comprehensive Thinking (Personal Credibility) – taking personal responsibility for mistakes; does what he/she commits to and is honest and forthright.
o	Willingness to Learn – handles change through taking advantage of lifelong learning opportunities and is open minded and curious to face new challenges.



ESSENTIAL JOB REQUIREMENTS:

•	Must be able to work occasional overtime
•	Must be able to sit for up to 4 hours at a time and 8 hours in a day
•	Punctuality and attendance in accordance with company policies

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
SYNOPSIS:  The Demand Planner is an individual contributor role that supports the effective planning and execution of materials and inventory to ensure product availability, cost efficiency, and on-time delivery. This role is responsible for maintaining accurate ERP parameters, analyzing demand and forecast data, executing purchase orders, and supporting inventory optimization efforts. The Demand Planner collaborates with cross-functional partners including Sales, Marketing, Operations, and Purchasing to maintain service targets and operational performance.

COMPETENCY PROFILE:
•	Global Competencies: Collaborate; Deliver Results; Develop Yourself; Drive Customer Value; Improve and Innovate; Take Ownership

RESPONSIBILITIES:
•	Execute inventory planning strategies to support improved inventory turns while maintaining required service levels. 
•	Prepare and maintain routine reports and data analysis to support key performance indicators (KPIs). 
•	Participate in Lean and continuous improvement activities aimed at enhancing productivity and supply chain efficiency. 
•	Monitor and manage inventory levels based on product usage, forecast trends, and customer stocking programs. 
•	Support quarterly inventory processes such as scrap reviews and returns to suppliers. 
•	Review customer demand, sales forecasts, and historical data to establish ERP system parameters (e.g., EOQ, lot size, safety stock, reorder points). 
•	Manage replenishment and stocking programs for designated customers. 
•	Collaborate with Marketing, Sales, and Strategic Purchasing to support stocking programs, bonded inventory, and blanket order execution. 
•	Track and report performance metrics related to product availability, delivery performance, inventory turns, and months-on-hand (MOH). 
•	Coordinate new item setups and data requirements with the Item Control Administrator. 
•	Provide cross-functional support and perform backup responsibilities within the Supply Chain Services (SCS) department as required. 
•	Attend meetings and prepare relevant planning data and reports. 
•	Perform daily ERP functions including: 
•	Creating purchase orders
•	Issuing orders
•	Preparing reject notices and corrective action requests
•	Responding to supplier issues related to forecasts, product demand, revisions, or receipt discrepancies
•	Other duties as assigned by manager

QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Bachelor’s Degree in Supply Chain Management, Business, Operations, or related field. 
•	3–5 years of relevant experience in demand planning, supply planning, or supply chain operations. 
•	Working knowledge of MRP and CRP systems. 
•	Experience using forecasting tools and understanding of bills of materials. 
•	Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint). 
•	Solid math and statistical analysis skills. 
•	Ability to manage multiple tasks with accuracy and attention to detail. 
•	Ability to work independently and collaboratively. 
•	Strong communication, problem-solving, and organizational skills. 
•	Self-motivated, action-oriented, and able to work in a fast-paced environment.

SUPPORTING QUALIFICATIONS:
•	APICS/ASCM certification (CPIM or CSCP).
•	Experience with SAP or equivalent ERP systems.
•	Understanding of Kanban, Lean principles, or continuous improvement methodologies.
•	Import/export coordination experience.
•	Programming or advanced analytical skills (e.g., SQL, VBA, or Power BI).
•	MBA or additional advanced education in Supply Chain is a plus (not required).

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	Must be able to withstand long periods (up to 4 hours without a break)
•	Must be able to read and write fluently in English
•	Must be professional, ethical, and customer-focused
•	Must be willing to travel internationally
•	Must be willing to work over-time as needed

metadata.value:
SYNOPSIS: 
The Field Application Engineer (FAE) will provide technical, hands-on expertise to support all business opportunities for Phoenix Contact products and applications directly associated with the automation, control and networking portfolio used within the industrial automation and electronics industry. The Field Application Engineer will collaborate with regional sales managers, strategic account managers, sales engineers, account development specialists, channel, and field application engineering teams, to utilize a consultative approach with all customers to support their individual needs and expand market penetration. This role plays a pivotal part in the development and expansion of all accounts, through automation, control and networking sales growth and specifications.  This position is an individual contributor role and does not include formal people management responsibilities.  

	COMPETENCY PROFILE: 
•	Global Competencies: Collaborate; Deliver Results; Develop Yourself; Drive Customer Value; Improve and Innovate; Take Ownership

RESPONSIBILITES:
•	Increase annual sales revenue across all products directly associated with the automation, control, and networking portfolio at customer accounts. These products include but are not limited to ethernet products (wired or wireless), safety, controllers, IO, HMIs, IPCs and engineered solutions.
•	Work in coordination with local Phoenix Contact sales team utilizing joint sales activities, trainings, and mutual action plans to advance the sales of above-mentioned products.
•	Act as the local technical representative for the above-mentioned products and support the sales team in power-related technical matters.
•	Act as the regional marketing specialist developing sales tools, collateral and driving campaigns.  
•	Serve as the “voice of the customer” providing insight for expansion of product lines or new focus industries.
•	Drive value-added applications and engineered solutions. 
•	Meet/exceed territory sales quotas as established by the RSM and/or Vice President - Field Sales.
•	Be proficient in BASE selling skills and implement to build strong customer relationships.
•	Develops and maintains strong relationships with potential clients, partners, and other stakeholders to facilitate new business and sustain growth. 
•	Maintain and update CRM systems with accurate client information. Fully utilize the CRM as a communication, reporting, measurement, and tracking tool to enhance productivity.
•	Effectively communicate with the entire Phoenix Contact team, both verbally and written.
•	Be proficient in and drive the use of all sales tools provided to support local markets.
•	Active involvement in industry-related associations.
•	Proactively utilize all resources and sales tools to maximize productivity, responsiveness, and customer delight.
•	Continue personal development through an effective skill-building program as determined by the RSM, Director of Sales, and/or the Harrisburg Training Department.
•	Demonstrate technical competence on all products/solutions within area of focus.
•	Provide reports to RSM as required.
•	Deliver presentations and hands-on demonstrations of Phoenix Contact automation, control and networking products, applications, and technology for the purpose of product sales.
•	Develop and maintain a working knowledge on all Phoenix Contact automation, control, and networking products/solutions.
•	Other duties as assigned.

QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Four-year college degree.
•	Technical discipline preferred and/or equivalent industry experience.
•	3 years minimum sales or marketing experience with automation and industrial control products.
•	Ability to work independently from home office and communicate effectively with colleagues with limited supervision.
•	Excellent oral/written communication and presentation skills.
•	Self-motivated with the ability to learn and adapt quickly.
•	Demonstrates a willingness to collaborate with others, build relationships, and involve others in decision making. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to deliver results by solving or supporting others in solving problems, prioritizing tasks, and/or resources, and focusing on outcomes. 
•	Demonstrates self-awareness and the willingness to learn and grow, accept changes, and effectively adapt to changing work demands. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to drive customer value through knowledge of the business, putting effort into identifying customer needs, and delivering work to maximize the value for customers. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to promote the value of improvement and innovation by staying ahead of trends, creating ideas and opportunities, and generating speed. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to take ownership by making decisions, taking responsibility, and driving things forward. 

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	Must be able to travel via airplane.
•	Must have a valid driver’s license and be able to travel via automobile for up to six hours at a time.
•	Must be able to lift a minimum of 50 lbs.
•	Must be able to travel overnight 40%+ or as dictated by assigned territory.
•	Comply with company dress code guidelines.
•	Punctuality is required.
•	Regular and on-time attendance.

SYNOPSIS:  The Sales Engineer is responsible for driving sales of technical and industrial products by combining engineering knowledge with strong interpersonal and business skills. This role involves identifying customer needs, recommending suitable products or solutions, and providing technical support throughout the sales process. The engineer works closely with manufacturing, R&D, and marketing teams to ensure customer satisfaction and achieve sales targets.  This role play a pivotal in the marketing, sales, customer service and business development required to promote and support the organization’s products.  Works to improve the organization’s market position and achieve financial growth. 

COMPETENCY PROFILE:  
•	Global Competencies: Collaborate; Deliver Results; Develop Yourself; Drive Customer Value; Improve and Innovate; Take Ownership
	
	RESPONSIBILITIES:
•	Meet/exceed territory sales quotas as established by the RSM and/or Director of Sales.
•	Be proficient in BASE selling skills and implement to build strong customer relationships.
•	Differentiate Phoenix Contact from other component vendors by building “partner” relationships with customers.
•	Focus on the customer by averaging a minimum of 16 to 20 sales calls per week, with at least 50% to assigned accounts.
•	Fully utilize Avenue as a communication, reporting, measurement and tracking tool to enhance productivity.
•	Effectively communicate with the entire Phoenix Contact team, both verbally and written.
•	Be proficient in and drive the use of all sales tools provided to support local markets.
•	Consistently provide feedback to the field and Harrisburg regarding territory requirements as it relates to personnel, product, and/or market requirements.
•	Work closely with Harrisburg Marketing to aid in the development of field presentations and application notes.
•	Active involvement in industry related associations.
•	Proactively utilize all resources and sales tools to maximize productivity, responsiveness, and customer delight.
•	Continue personal development through an effective skill building program as determined by the RSM, Director of Sales, and/or the Harrisburg Training Department.
•	Demonstrate technical competence on all products/solutions within area of focus.
•	Provide forecasts and reports to RSM as required.
•	Develop and implement effective sales plans across a geographic territory as defined by regional management.
•	Independently and/or in conjunction with regional management, develop and implement effective Mutual Action Plans with assigned distributor locations.
•	Work independently or in conjunction with regional management to plan, document, measure and grow assigned distributor locations.
•	Deliver presentations and hands-on demonstrations of Phoenix Contact products, applications, and technology for the purpose of product sales.
•	Where applicable, work with National Accounts Team and Harrisburg Marketing to implement global/national account strategies in order to achieve program objectives.
•	Develop and maintain a working knowledge on all Phoenix Contact products/solutions.
•	Qualify and follow-up on all leads within assigned territory and/or account portfolio.
•	Follow-up on all quotations within assigned territory and/or account portfolio.
•	Responsible for all sales related activities within assigned territory, with a focus on all control cabinet, machine mounted, and industrial networking solutions.
•	Drive value-added/custom solutions within assigned account base, comprised primarily of industrial OEMs, local end users and project business.
•	Become proficient on all control cabinet, machine mounted and industrial networking applications and our corresponding solutions, to the point of being considered a consultant by the market.
•	Other duties as assigned.
	
	QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Four-year degree.
•	Technical discipline preferred and/or equivalent industrial industry experience.
•	3 to 5 years’ minimum sales experience in the electronics marketplace.
•	Experience working with distribution preferred.
•	Demonstrates a willingness to collaborate with others, build relationships, and involve others in decision making. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to deliver results by solving or supporting others in solving problems, prioritizing tasks, and/or resources, and focusing on outcomes. 
•	Demonstrates self-awareness and the willingness to learn and grow, accept changes, and effectively adapt to changing work demands. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to drive customer value through knowledge of the business, putting effort into identifying customer needs, and delivering work to maximize the value for customers. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to promote the value of improvement and innovation by staying ahead of trends, creating ideas and opportunities, and generating speed. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to take ownership by making decisions, taking responsibility, and driving things forward. 

	ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	Must be able to travel via airplane.
•	Must have a valid driver’s license and be able to travel via automobile for up to six hours at a time.
•	Must be able to lift a minimum of 50 lbs.
•	Must be able to travel overnight 20-25% as dictated by assigned territory.
•	Comply with company dress code guidelines.
•	Punctuality is required.
•	Regular and on-time attendance.

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
SYNOPSIS:
We are seeking a passionate and strategic (Senior Product Marketing Specialist I) to lead the promotion of our Board-to-Board connectors across key markets. This role blends technical advocacy, collateral development, and product strategy to drive awareness, customer engagement, and adoption. This position bridges technical expertise and marketing strategy by supporting product launches, training, and promotional activities across internal teams, distributors, and customers. The role also contributes to the coordination and execution of marketing plans, advertising campaigns, tradeshows, and technical support initiatives. 
Reporting to the Manager of Product – Device Connectivity, the specialist collaborates closely with internal stakeholders, sales teams, and distribution partners to advance network product solutions and ensure successful market engagement.  This position is an individual contributor role and does not include formal people management responsibilities.  The title “Senior” reflects responsibility for supporting processes, programs, or strategic initiatives, not people.  Individual Contributor Senior related roles are often subject matter experts who lead cross-functional efforts, influence decisions, and own outcomes, but do not supervise direct reports.

COMPETENCY PROFILE: 
•	Global Competencies: Collaborate; Deliver Results; Develop Yourself; Drive Customer Value; Improve and Innovate; Take Ownership

JOB RESPONSIBILITIES:
•	Assist in the execution of marketing plans, programs, pricing, and sales efforts within the areas of product focus 
•	Develop technical and sales enablement materials for internal teams, distributors, and customers 
•	Implement and coordinate trade show demos / designs and participate in trade show activities
•	Deliver product training to internal teams, distributors, and customers, focusing on product features, benefits, and applications 
•	Utilize social media (e.g., LinkedIn) to drive awareness of Phoenix Contact products and services as well as to build connections within the industry and potential customer base
•	Answer technical questions relative to the area of product focus for Phoenix Contact personnel, manufacturer’s representatives, authorized distributors, and customers
•	Drive pricing and quotation activities by setting competitive pricing structures, analyzing market trends, and reviewing special pricing requests 
•	Research products and industries including competitive and market trends
•	Assist with inventory control and coordination with emphasis on new product launches
•	Coordinate on customer projects and communicate effectively with Blomberg HQ product management, and the US based solutions team
•	Other duties as assigned.
QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Business Administration or Engineering Bachelor degree or equivalent technical work experience.
•	Must have a basic understanding of factory automation and industrial applications.
•	Prefer five+ sales and/or marketing experience with connectors and cable assemblies.
•	Working knowledge of applications within Microsoft 365.
•	Excellent communication and presentation skills. 
•	Detailed reporting skills and analysis insight.
•	Demonstrates a willingness to collaborate with others, build relationships, and involve others in decision making. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to deliver results by solving or supporting others in solving problems, prioritizing tasks, and/or resources, and focusing on outcomes. 
•	Demonstrates self-awareness and the willingness to learn and grow, accept changes, and effectively adapt to changing work demands. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to drive customer value through knowledge of the business, putting effort into identifying customer needs, and delivering work to maximize the value for customers. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to promote the value of improvement and innovation by staying ahead of trends, creating ideas and opportunities, and generating speed. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to take ownership by making decisions, taking responsibility, and driving things forward. 

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	20–25% travel required, including occasional overseas travel.
•	Must be able to lift 50 pounds.
•	Must be able to sit and/or stand for extended periods (2–4 hours at a time).
•	Must be able to work on a computer for extended periods (up to 4 hours at a time).
•	Must be able to travel via automobile, train, or plane for up to 10 hours.
•	Must be able to communicate effectively in verbal and written English.
•	Regular and on-time attendance. 
•	This is a hybrid position requiring at least three days in the office.

jobs.title: Senior Product Marketing Specialist I – Board to Board Connectivity

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
SYNOPSIS:
Working under general direction the Project Manager role will be responsible for planning and overseeing projects within the US IT organization, working with individual(s) and/or group(s) across Phoenix Contact, both locally and globally.  Success will be characterized by, but not limited to, delivery of projects that are on time, within budget, and within scope, meeting project objectives and helping the organization realize project benefits, effective communication and organization of concurrent activities, collaboration across multiple teams, and alignment with corporate processes and guidelines.

COMPETENCY PROFILE:
•	Global Competencies: Collaborate; Deliver Results; Develop Yourself; Drive Customer Value; Improve and Innovate; Take Ownership
•	General Competencies: Attention to Communication; Fostering Teamwork; Establishing Focus/Strategic Thinking; Forward Thinking; Results Orientation; Fostering Innovation
•	Technical Competencies: Project Integration Management; Project Scope Management; Project Time Management; Project Cost Management; Project Quality Management; Project Human Resources Management; Project Communications Management; Project Risk Management; Project Procurement Management; Project Stakeholder Management

RESPONSIBILITIES:
•	Works with team managers to define resource requirements, manage resource availability and allocation, both internal and third party.
•	Outlines project budgets based on requirements and tracks costs to deliver projects on budget.
•	Prepares detailed project plans to schedule key project milestones, critical path, workstreams, and activities.
•	Tracks and monitors projects from initiation through delivery, interfacing with all technical and non-technical team members, extended team members and stakeholders.
•	Provides regular status reports to project teams, key stakeholders and others to be consulted and informed.
•	Manages and adjusts baselines and timeline for any changes in project scope, schedule, and/or budget.
•	Identifies and communicates risks and tracks/manages their mitigation.
•	Manages relationships and communications with clients and/or stakeholders, ensuring projects are delivered to the satisfaction of these groups.
•	Works effectively in a matrix organization with IT service delivery managers and colleagues within the global DPS organization.
•	Assists in determination and delivery of IT project needs, capacity planning, prioritization of projects and essential activities as needed and necessary.  
•	Aligns with global DPS/IT Project Management Milestone processes
•	Collaborates with Program Managers as necessary for project and strategic planning
•	Maintains pertinent documentation (including but not limited to): 
o	Requirements and specifications
o	Risk lists, mitigation, and contingency plans
o	Milestone checklists
•	Work with other Project Managers on PMO process enhancements and standardization on the project toolkit with input and feedback from other IT leaders.
•	Execute as knowledge leader in project management on project tools, concepts, and standards for best practice. 
•	Other duties as assigned by one’s Manager

QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Bachelor’s degree in in Information Systems or Business field of study with at least 3 years experience leading projects.  Or 8 years of IT project management experience.
•	5 (+) years of relevant professional-level work experience preferred.
•	Certified Project Management Professional (PMP), or the ability to achieve certification within first year. 
•	Strong background in IT disciplines and related architecture 
•	Professional experience with both Agile and Waterfall Project Management methodology.
•	Demonstrated ability to adapt to diverse cultures, time zones, and related factors.
•	Excellent presentation and business communication skills for executive, managerial, and individual contributor staff levels.
•	Professional attitude and well organized.
•	Self-starting, self-motivated, self-confident individual, open to taking on challenges and finding ways to succeed.
•	Proficient with Windows based office applications and project management tools.
•	Excellent people skills, easy to work with, and the ability to motivate others.
•	Demonstrates leadership qualities.
•	Must be able to work in a global team environment.
•	Must display a very high degree of adaptability and flexibility to work jointly with others and accept suggestions and new ideas.
•	Demonstrates a willingness to collaborate with others, build relationships, and involve others in decision making.  
•	Demonstrates the ability to deliver results by solving or supporting others in solving problems, prioritizing tasks and/or resources, and focusing on outcomes.  
•	Demonstrates self-awareness and the willingness to learn and grow, accept changes, and effectively adapt to changing work demands.  
•	Demonstrates the ability to drive customer value through knowledge of the business, putting effort into identifying customer needs, and delivering work to maximize the value for customers.  
•	Demonstrates a willingness to promote the value of improvement and innovation by staying ahead of trends, creating ideas and opportunities, and generating speed.  
•	Demonstrates a willingness to take ownership by making decisions, taking responsibility, and driving things forward.  

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	Must be able to travel domestically and overseas for extended periods if required.
o	Must be eligible to receive a US passport
o	Must be able to travel by air
o	Must be able to ride in a car for an extended time
•	Must be able to sit for extended periods of time.
•	Must be able to communicate in English (written & verbal).
•	Regular and on-time attendance.
•	This is a hybrid position requiring at least three days in the office.

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
SYNOPSIS: The Software Development Engineer II position is the individual contributor intermediate level position in the software engineering series. Under moderate supervision, the incumbent will support all software aspects of the development and design of products and product improvements of moderate difficulty requiring the use of some independent judgment. Incidental to these design efforts, the Software Development Engineer II will work both independently and as part of a team to develop creative solutions based on requirements from specific customers or general market requirements.  

	COMPETENCY PROFILE: 
•	Global Competencies: Collaborate; Deliver Results; Develop Yourself; Drive Customer Value; Improve and Innovate; Take Ownership

RESPONSIBILITES:
•	Identify where product features interfere with usability or maintainability
•	Understands and follows product development processes
•	Create introductory installation for small applications and utilities
•	Integrate software components into existing build
•	Develops using software best practices to ensure implementation quality
•	Adds component to the repository
•	Reproduces and defines boundaries of defect, suggest causes or dependencies
•	Can retrieve and review project documentation in EPO
•	Ability to work with code that is object oriented, modular, and readable
•	Using source control tools and complier
•	Working knowledge of assemblies
•	Ability to understand and run previously written scripts and modify as required
•	Knowledge or familiarity of architecture within product
•	Ability to understand and run unit test previously written and modify as required
•	Implementing specific user interface detail
•	Other duties as assigned by manager.

QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Experience with C++ and C# software development
•	BS Degree in Software Engineering/Science or comparable preferred
•	2-5 years of software experience 
•	Tactical group written/verbal communication to peers/management
•	Works within framework of identified organizational strategy
•	Work as directed with Senior level personnel/managers 
•	Meets 100% of Development Engineer (Software) Level II Competency Profile
•	Must be eligible to obtain passport
•	Demonstrates a willingness to collaborate with others, build relationships, and involve others in decision making. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to deliver results by solving or supporting others in solving problems, prioritizing tasks, and/or resources, and focusing on outcomes. 
•	Demonstrates self-awareness and the willingness to learn and grow, accept changes, and effectively adapt to changing work demands. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to drive customer value through knowledge of the business, putting effort into identifying customer needs, and delivering work to maximize the value for customers. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to promote the value of improvement and innovation by staying ahead of trends, creating ideas and opportunities, and generating speed. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to take ownership by making decisions, taking responsibility, and driving things forward. 

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	Must be able to travel by air
•	Occasional overseas travel for extended periods required
•	Occasional Domestic Travel required
•	Must be able to sit for extended periods of time
•	Must be able to communicate effectively in English
•	Must be able to lift 25 lbs.
•	Regular and on-time attendance.
•	This is a hybrid position unless otherwise specified requiring 3 days in the office.

metadata.value:
SYNOPSIS:	
Solution Engineer I (SE) serve as strategic technical consultants and project leaders, supporting Automation Solutions sales, applied system development, and project initiatives across focused vertical markets. Operating with a high level of autonomy, SEs design, implement, and support advanced solutions that integrate Phoenix Contact’s complex technical products with third-party components to meet specific customer or industry segment needs. These specialists interpret and translate customer specifications into functional concepts, delivering tailored application solutions that address operational challenges and align with industry best practices.
Industries served include oil and gas, water/wastewater treatment, building automation, data centers, transportation infrastructure, electric power, automotive, and network & security. Solution Engineers are strategically positioned to drive innovation, organizational agility, and long-term business value.

COMPETENCY PROFILE: 
•	Global Competencies: Initiative; Comprehensive Thinking (Personal Credibility); Collaboration; Willingness to Learn 

RESPONSIBILITIES:
•	Develop an expert area of specialization (industry/technology) as directed by manager and attain related certifications
•	Mentor and advise Associate Solution Engineers, Technical Service, Product Engineering, and Product Management on product performance in the field and suggest design changes to improve product performance and customer satisfaction
•	Design training programs and training material to support complex control system projects
•	Develop advanced system testing and troubleshooting processes utilizing spectrum analyzers, storage scopes, power monitors, volt meters, and other required test equipment
•	Provide applied solution design and engineering support to the Control and Industry Solutions team
•	Independently qualify and gather input for designs through functional specification review or by direct customer interaction
•	Provide pre-sales technical support and develop a concept framework based on customer requirements and industry standards and practices
•	Develop and implement test plans to validate proposed solutions
•	Develop and serve as technical lead on ‘proof of concept’ or pilot applications
•	Provide project management as required, and advise project team on technical issues throughout the project lifecycle
•	Develop, implement, and provide on-site installation start-up support
•	Perform application demonstrations together with Phoenix Contact sales engineer and/or PSE at potential customer sites
•	Research and understand existing competitive and emerging products and technologies
•	Document and internally communicate data supporting the development of new products to meet focus market/customer requirements
•	Support the sales effort of Phoenix Contact’s complex technical products when positioned and sold as industry solutions at OEM, end-user, and system integrator focused accounts within primary and supported market segments
•	Support Phoenix Contact field personnel and channel partners in technical aspects relating to the sale of systems and solutions
•	Develop and conduct control solution presentations and technology seminars to targeted audiences
•	Position Phoenix Contact as a technology leader through active involvement in industry related technical associations
•	Develop and maintain an expert understanding of industrial networking and communication technologies.  Specific expertise in Industrial Ethernet Protocols:  Profinet, Ethernet/IP, and Modbus TCP
•	Develop and maintain an expert working knowledge on all Phoenix Contact industrial products with specialized emphasis on IEC 61131 programming, industrial ethernet (wired and wireless), and networked I/O
•	Maintain knowledge proficiency through continued participation in industry trade events and conferences including active participation in presenting Phoenix Contact Case Studies, etc.
•	Attendance and participation in accordance with corporate policies in local, national, and international meetings
•	Keeps others informed during projects/work efforts by sharing relevant ideas and information
•	Solves problems in a timely manner by identifying customer needs and/or satisfaction levels
•	Builds mutually beneficial relationships focused on assistance, cooperation, information sharing, and support
•	Gains support for win-win solutions that address the needs of those involved
•	Builds strong relationships and optimal results by employing the winning solutions value-based approach and techniques 
•	Looks for and seizes profitable business opportunities
•	Introduces or develops new and improved methods, products, procedures, or technologies
•	Demonstrates knowledge and/or ability in a technical area
•	Demonstrates initiative by understanding what actions need to be taken and completes these tasks while seeking the perspective of others as required
•	Demonstrates an honest and forthright presence by taking responsibility for own mistakes and not blaming others, and by doing what he/she commits to doing
•	Works as a team player to make a difference within the business and expresses gratitude and appreciation to others who have provided information, assistance, or support
•	Readily handles change through taking advantage of lifelong learning opportunities and being open minded and curious in the face of new challenges
•	Other duties as assigned by the Manager

QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Bachelor’s degree in engineering or technical focus preferred
•	BSEE/EET/MFET/Computer Engineering
•	Minimum associate’s technical degree and equivalent experience
•	Minimum of 4 years industrial automation experience                                 
•	Certified PROFINET Network Engineer
•	Industry specific controls system design and programming experience
•	Motivated self-starter with the ability to work autonomously and as part of a team
•	Ability to set priorities and perform simultaneous tasks
•	Ability to meet deadlines and adjust to changing priorities to meet business goals
•	Excellent written and verbal communication skills
•	Proficient computer skills (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
•	Effective interpersonal skills with comfort in delivering group presentations
•	Advanced/Expert level knowledge in one or more of the following areas:
•	IEC 61131 programming software (PC Worx, CodeSys, PLCNext Engineer)
•	HMI/SCADA applications and software
•	Ethernet (Modbus/TCP, Ethernet/IP, Profinet, IEC 61850, DNP3)
•	Wireless (900mHz, 802.11, Bluetooth) – ability to conduct site surveys
•	Fieldbus (INTERBUS, Profibus, DeviceNet, etc.)
•	Safety (controllers, relays, bridge, IS I/O)
•	Demonstrate initiative by understanding what actions need to be taken and completing the required tasks while seeking the perspective of others as required
•	Demonstrate an honest and forthright presence by taking responsibility for own mistakes and not blaming others, and by doing what he / she commits to doing
•	Work as a team player to make a difference within the business and express gratitude and appreciation to others who have provided information, assistance, or support
•	Readily handle change through taking advantage of lifelong learning opportunities and being open minded and curious in the face of new challenges
•	Demonstrates a willingness to collaborate with others, build relationships, and involve others in decision making. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to deliver results by solving or supporting others in solving problems, prioritizing tasks, and/or resources, and focusing on outcomes. 
•	Demonstrates self-awareness and the willingness to learn and grow, accept changes, and effectively adapt to changing work demands. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to drive customer value through knowledge of the business, putting effort into identifying customer needs, and delivering work to maximize the value for customers. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to promote the value of improvement and innovation by staying ahead of trends, creating ideas and opportunities, and generating speed. Demonstrates a willingness to take ownership by making decisions, taking responsibility, and driving things forward. 

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	35% Travel Requirement (2% - 5% Overseas)
•	Must be able to lift 60 pounds
•	Must be able to sit and/or stand for extended periods of time (2-4 hours at a time)
•	Must be able to work on a computer for extended periods of time (up to 4 hours at a time)
•	Must be able to travel in vehicles (automobiles, trains, and planes) for up to 10 hours
•	Must be able to communicate effectively in verbal and written English
•	Must comply with company dress code guidelines
•	Punctuality and attendance in accordance with company policies
•	Timely reporting of expenses
•	Regular and on-time attendance. 
•	This is a hybrid position requiring at least three days in the office, unless otherwise specified.

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
This is a hands-on, activity-driven role ideal for someone who is eager to learn, contribute, and grow within a dynamic team responsible for driving sales of all Phoenix Contact products by combining engineering knowledge with strong interpersonal and business skills. The Strategic Account Manager (SAM) plays a key role on the regional sales team by providing leadership and direction in the establishment, development, and growth of assigned Regional Strategic Accounts. This position will carry out responsibilities in all of the following functions and areas: creating strategic plans, communicating direction, implementing corporate business reviews, creating corporate road-maps, expanding relationships (Maintain and develop relationships at all levels of Management, Procurement, Operations, and Quality Assurance, Engineering, Sales, and Estimating/quotations departments), analysis and recommendations, corporate price negotiations, and leading cross-functional teams.
Responsibilities
•	Meet or exceed sales quotas as established by the RSM and/or Vice President—Field Sales.
•	Be proficient in BASE selling skills and implement to build strong customer relationships.
•	Lead the charge in development of a strategic plan and implementation of strategic initiatives for regional strategic accounts. The focus includes increasing account share, revenue growth, profitability, building and expanding relationships with major stakeholders, increasing product mix, and expanding the Phoenix Contact brand.
•	Develop strategic roadmaps for strategic assigned accounts providing direction and insight on revenue generating locations. 
•	Develop intimate insight into the customer culture, needs, and supply chain, including key metrics leading to the recommendation of plans, initiatives, and/or corrective actions to provide the highest level of service to the customer’s organization.
•	Lead and coordinate established business reviews with the assigned strategic accounts, including monitoring of competitive activity, leading to recommendations to the Phoenix Contact team on areas of improvement.
•	Organize and lead cross functional teams with the appropriate members of Phoenix Contact for the purpose of building and maintaining trusting relationships between Phoenix Contact and assigned strategic accounts.
•	Support the preparation of proposals, presentations, and sales materials.
•	Maintain and update CRM systems with accurate client information. Fully utilize the CRM as a communication, reporting, measurement, and tracking tool to enhance productivity.
•	Collaborate with the entire Phoenix Contact team to align efforts, effectively communicate both verbally and written.
•	Track and report on business development activities and performance metrics.
•	Differentiate Phoenix Contact from other component vendors by building “partner” relationships with customers.
•	Be proficient in and drive the use of all sales tools provided to support local markets.
•	Work closely with Harrisburg Product teams to aid in the development of field presentations and application notes.
•	Proactively utilize all resources and sales tools to maximize productivity, responsiveness, and customer delight.
•	Continue personal development through an effective skill-building program as determined by the RSM, Director of Sales, and/or the Harrisburg Training Department.
•	Demonstrate technical competence on all products/solutions within the area of focus.
•	Provide forecasts and reports to RSM as required.
•	Deliver presentations and hands-on demonstrations of Phoenix Contact products, applications, and technology for the purpose of product sales.
•	Develop and maintain a working knowledge on all Phoenix Contact products/solutions.
•	Qualify and follow up on all opportunities within the assigned strategic accounts.
•	Follow up on all quotations within the assigned strategic account portfolio.
Qualifications
•	Four-year degree preferred.
•	Technical discipline preferred and/or equivalent experience.
•	5+ years minimum sales experience in the electronics or industrial automation & controls marketplace.
•	Experience working with distribution preferred.
•	Excellent networking, and influencing skills needed to communicate at all levels.
•	Exhibits high-level interpersonal and negotiation skills.
•	Successful history in achieving strategic and sales targets.
•	Self-motivated with the ability to learn and adapt quickly.
Essential Job Functions
•	Must be able to travel via airplane.
•	Must have a valid driver’s license and be able to travel via automobile for up to six hours at a time.
•	Must be able to lift a minimum of 50 lbs.
•	Must be able to travel overnight 35%+ or as dictated by assigned strategic account locations.
•	Comply with company dress code guidelines.
•	Punctuality is required.
•	Regular and on-time attendance.

•	Follow up on all quotations within the assigned strategic account portfolio.
Qualifications
•	Four-year degree preferred.
•	Technical discipline preferred and/or equivalent experience.
•	5+ years minimum sales experience in the electronics or industrial automation & controls marketplace.
•	Experience working with distribution preferred.
•	Excellent networking, and influencing skills needed to communicate at all levels.
•	Exhibits high-level interpersonal and negotiation skills.
•	Successful history in achieving strategic and sales targets.
•	Self-motivated with the ability to learn and adapt quickly.
Essential Job Functions
•	Must be able to travel via airplane.
•	Must have a valid driver’s license and be able to travel via automobile for up to six hours at a time.
•	Must be able to lift a minimum of 50 lbs.
•	Must be able to travel overnight 35%+ or as dictated by assigned strategic account locations.
•	Comply with company dress code guidelines.
•	Punctuality is required.
•	Regular and on-time attendance.

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
SYNOPSIS:
•	The position provides leadership and direction in the establishment, development, and growth of assigned Strategic Accounts within the Account Based Sales pillar. This position will carry out responsibilities in all of the following functions and areas: creating strategic plans, communicating direction, implementing corporate business reviews, creating corporate road-maps, expanding relationships (Maintain and develop relationships at all levels of Management, Procurement, Operations, and Quality Assurance, Engineering, Sales, and Estimating/quotations departments), analysis and recommendations, corporate price negotiations, and leading cross-functional teams.

COMPETENCY PROFILE:
•	Global Competencies: Initiative; Comprehensive Thinking (Personal Credibility); Collaboration; Willingness to Learn
•	Job-Specific Competencies: Building Collaborative (Partnership) Relationships; Consultative Selling; Influencing Others; Strategic Thinking; Technical Expertise; Entrepreneurial Orientation; Business Acumen

RESPONSIBILITIES:
•	Lead the charge in the development of a strategic plan and implementation of strategic initiatives for assigned Data Center customers. The focus includes increasing account share, revenue growth, profitability, building and expanding relationships with major stakeholders, increasing product mix, and expanding the Phoenix Contact brand.
•	Develop strategic roadmaps for assigned accounts providing direction and insight on existing revenue generating locations and prioritization of developmental locations.
•	Develop intimate insight into the customer culture, needs, and supply chain, including key metrics leading to the recommendation of plans, initiatives, and/or corrective actions to provide the highest level of service to the customer’s organization.
•	Lead and coordinate established business reviews with the assigned accounts, including monitoring of competitive activity, leading to recommendations to the Phoenix Contact team on areas of improvement.
•	Organize and lead cross functional teams with the appropriate members of Phoenix Contact for the purpose of building and maintaining trusting relationships between Phoenix Contact and assigned accounts.
•	Manage project transitions assigned to specific locations as projects develop (e.g., specification, bid, procurement, and implementation) and oversee health of the opportunity pipeline.
•	Lead the development of presentations and proposals for assigned customers, soliciting support as applicable from necessary divisional resources.
•	Communicate price negotiations / actions for assigned accounts to the Harrisburg, Blomberg, and other Subsidiary teams including the integration of the channel.
•	Utilize current CRM as the primary customer planning, reporting, measurement, and opportunity tracking tool.
•	Be proficient in and drive the use of all sales tools provided to support local markets.
•	Active involvement in industry related associations, as well as distributor, and customer events.
•	Demonstrate technical competence on all products/solutions within area of focus.
•	Maintain all corporate policies, procedures, and programs including quotations, lead follow-up, and timely reporting.
•	Where applicable, work with channel partners and Harrisburg Marketing to implement national account strategies to achieve program objectives.
•	Assist in the sales promotion of packaged control systems and end-to-end solutions created by the AEP team (Application Engineering & Projects).
•	Other duties as assigned.

QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Bachelor’s degree preferred or equivalent combination of education and experience in a technical discipline.
•	5+ years of proven success in Industrial Controls and Automation Sales required.
•	Experience with Data Center, systems design, components, construction, commissioning, or service required.
•	Experience in working with global and high-profile accounts preferred.
•	Proficiency in R3/BASE selling skills and the ability to build mutually beneficial customer relationships.
•	Demonstrated competence in establishing and leading of cross-functional teams.
•	Excellent networking, interpersonal and influencing skills needed to communicate at all levels.
•	Successful history in achieving strategic and sales targets.
•	Experience managing sales through distribution channel a plus.
•	Outstanding communication skills – written, verbal and listening.
•	Self-motivated with the ability to learn and adapt quickly.
•	Exhibits high-level interpersonal and negotiation skills.
•	Demonstrates initiative by understanding what actions need to be taken and completes these tasks while seeking the perspective of others as required.
•	Demonstrates an honest and forthright presence by taking responsibility for own mistakes and not blaming others, and by doing what he/she commits to doing.
•	Works as a team player to get results within the business and expresses gratitude and appreciation to others who have provided information, assistance, or support.
•	Demonstrates a willingness to collaborate with others, build relationships, and involve others in decision making.
•	Demonstrates the ability to deliver results by solving or supporting others in solving problems, prioritizing tasks, and/or resources, and focusing on outcomes.
•	Demonstrates self-awareness and the willingness to learn and grow, accept changes, and effectively adapt to changing work demands.
•	Demonstrates the ability to drive customer value through knowledge of the business, putting effort into identifying customer needs, and delivering work to maximize the value for customers.
•	Demonstrates a willingness to promote the value of improvement and innovation by staying ahead of trends, creating ideas and opportunities, and generating speed.
•	Demonstrates a willingness to take ownership by making decisions, taking responsibility, and driving things forward.
•	Readily adapts to change through taking advantage of lifelong learning opportunities and being open minded and curious in the face of new challenges.

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	Must be able to travel via airplane.
•	Must have a valid driver’s license and be able to operate an automobile for up to six hours at a time.
•	Must be able to lift a minimum of 50 lbs. (sample, demos, literature)
•	Must be able to travel overnight 35% + or as required by customer location.
•	Some international travel – valid passport required.
•	Comply with company dress code guidelines.
•	Regular and on-time attendance.

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
SYNOPSIS:
The Strategic Purchasing Buyer is an individual contributor role responsible for supplier evaluation, qualification, and management, cost reduction, sourcing, supply risk management, spend analytics, ERP/MRP utilization, and troubleshooting. This position will work closely with the Suppliers, tactical Buyers, Engineers, and Finance to provide an efficient and cost-effective supply chain, requiring a well-rounded understanding of our products, production, processes, and supply base capabilities. 

COMPETENCY PROFILE: 
•	Global Competencies: Collaborate; Deliver Results; Develop Yourself; Drive Customer Value; Improve and Innovate; Take Ownership 

RESPONSIBILITIES:
•	Resolves vendor or contractor grievances and claims against suppliers.
•	Develops KPIs of quality and delivery performance of assigned commodities and suppliers.  
•	Conducts cost analysis and negotiations for the pricing of purchased items.
•	Leads efforts related to cost savings & MCV (Material Cost Variance) targets to support corporate targets.
•	Develops supplier contracts for assigned commodities.
•	Performs risk management activities of assigned commodities.
•	Leads quarterly business reviews with strategic suppliers of assigned market supply segment.
•	Collaborates with Global Purchasing of assigned market supply segments to leverage global spend and supplier technological advancements.
•	Research market and economic trends that influence the assigned market supply segments.
•	Develops and executes strategic commodity plans for key commodities.
•	Establishes and maintains current master data in ERP system for suppliers and purchased items.
•	Completes the placement of new product and specialized purchase order requests to the supply base.
•	Contributes to the maintenance of “Approved Supplier List”.
•	Completes supplier qualification and development.
•	Collaborates with production planning to support the Material Disposition process. 
•	Interfaces with various internal functions such as Operations, Engineering, Accounting, and Quality to provide Strategic Purchasing support. 
•	Escalates expedites within the supplier’s organization when business unit expedites are not resolved.
•	Establishes special supplier managed inventory programs to improve turns and reduce inventory.
•	Provides purchasing support to New Product Development Engineering teams.
•	Attends production and design review meetings.
•	Participates in the Supplier Audit Program.
•	Supports LEAN six sigma efforts to improve process efficiency.
•	Other duties as assigned.

QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Business, or another relevant field.
•	3 (+) years’ relevant professional-level work experience preferred.
•	Demonstrated ability implementing strategic projects to support corporate goals.
•	Demonstrated ability working under pressure and handling multiple tasks.
•	Mathematical and statistical analysis skills.
•	Detail oriented with high level of accuracy.
•	Demonstrated ability communicating effectively at all levels of an organization.
•	Refined knowledge of and skill in MS Office Suite.
•	Knowledge of and skill in ERP procurement modules.
•	Demonstrates a willingness to collaborate with others, build relationships, and involve others in decision making.
•	Demonstrates the ability to deliver results by solving or supporting others in solving problems, prioritizing tasks and/or resources, and focusing on outcomes.
•	Demonstrates self-awareness and the willingness to learn and grow, accept changes, and effectively adapt to changing work demands.
•	Demonstrates the ability to drive customer value through knowledge of the business, putting effort into identifying customer needs, and delivering work to maximize the value for customers.
•	Demonstrates a willingness to promote the value of improvement and innovation by staying ahead of trends, creating ideas and opportunities, and generating speed.
•	Demonstrates a willingness to take ownership by making decisions, taking responsibility, and driving things forward.

SUPPORTING QUALIFICATIONS:
•	CPSM or CPM certification.

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	Domestic & International travel required.
•	Must be able to sit for extended periods of time.
•	Must be able to lift 40 pounds.
•	Must be able to speak and understand English to communicate verbally and in writing.
